name: Angelina County Teachers
description_short: State-chartered credit union serving Angelina County school employees and families since 1954. Offers vehicle loans, personal loans, checking, savings, and digital banking.
description_long:
Angelina County Teachers Credit Union (ACTCU) was founded in 1954 by a small group of educators from Lufkin Independent School District, with Mr. Pop Ward and Miss Zelma Cook playing instrumental roles in its establishment. Over seven decades, it has grown into a state-chartered institution answering to the Texas Credit Union Department, serving school employees across multiple districts in Angelina County. The organization operates on a member-owned, not-for-profit model with volunteer Board of Directors overseeing operations.

ACTCU provides a comprehensive suite of financial products including vehicle loans (as low as 6.00% APR), personal loans (as low as 12.00% APR), RV loans (as low as 6.00% APR), checking accounts, debit cards, savings accounts, and online banking services. Members enjoy 24/7 digital banking access through their mobile app and website platform. The credit union emphasizes personalized financial services tailored to member needs, with a stated mission to provide safe, sound, and competitive financial services in a responsible, caring, and professional manner.

What distinguishes ACTCU is its focused membership eligibility tied to Angelina County school districts (Lufkin ISD, Hudson, Central, Diboll, Zavalla, Huntington, plus Angelina College and PCA), creating a tight-knit community of educators and their families. Savings are fully insured by the National Credit Union Association (NCUA) up to $250,000. The credit union maintains a single physical location in Lufkin with limited weekday hours (9:30 AM–5:00 PM Monday–Friday) and relies heavily on digital banking for member convenience. There is a $35 membership fee (cash or check only) and membership requires employment verification via driver's license and Social Security card.

ACTCU represents a stable, community-focused financial institution with competitive loan rates and strong consumer protections. However, membership is restricted to specific school district employees and their families, and the physical branch presence is minimal. Digital banking capabilities appear robust, but limited branch hours may inconvenience members needing in-person service outside standard business hours. The organization's longevity and mission-driven approach suggest reliability, though rates and terms should be compared against other credit unions and lenders.
services: In-person membership application at branch location
pros: Competitive vehicle loan rates starting at 6.00% APR as of May 2025
Member savings insured by NCUA up to $250,000
Free 24/7 digital banking access via mobile app and online portal for enrolled members
State-chartered institution answering to Texas Credit Union Department regulatory oversight
Established 1954 with 70+ years of operational history and member trust
Personalized financial services model with volunteer member-led Board of Directors
Serves immediate family members of eligible school employees, expanding membership beyond direct staff
cons: Restrictive membership eligibility limited to Angelina County school district employees—cannot serve general public
Single physical branch location (402 S Medford Dr, Lufkin) limits accessibility for dispersed members
Limited operating hours (9:30 AM–5:00 PM weekdays only) may not accommodate working professionals
Non-refundable $35 membership fee plus requirement to bring physical ID and Social Security card in person
Personal loan rates (12.00% APR minimum) are substantially higher than vehicle loan rates, limiting affordability for non-auto needs
best_for: Angelina County school district employees (teachers, staff) seeking stable, member-focused credit union banking
Educators and school employees planning vehicle purchases with competitive sub-6% financing
Families of school district employees who qualify for membership and want NCUA-insured savings
Members preferring community-based credit union model over larger commercial banks
diagnosis: ACTCU is best for Angelina County school employees and their immediate families seeking stable, competitive-rate credit union services with strong regulatory oversight and NCUA insurance protection. The main caveat is strict membership eligibility tied to specific school districts—this credit union cannot serve the general public and requires in-person enrollment with physical documentation.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_description: Angelina County Teachers — NCUA-insured credit union in LUFKIN, TX. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
